description: In 2017, the Philippines government suspended the use of the Sanofi-Pasteur Dengvaxia vaccine after the manufacturer announced it could worsen symptoms in individuals who had not previously contracted dengue.

key_events.description: Sanofi admits the vaccine may pose risks for seronegative individuals.
Philippine Department of Health halts the nationwide immunization program.
Public protests erupt, citing fears for children who had already received the shot.
Government launches criminal investigation into previous health administration officials.

calibration_notes: Examines the geopolitical fallout and erosion of trust in national vaccination programs following a localized safety crisis.
